trading system that predicts whether **BTC will go UP or DOWN over the next 15 minutes**.\n\nThe task is strictly:\n\n- **Asset:** BTC\n\n- **Prediction type:** binary directional classification (**UP / DOWN**)\n\n- **Forecast horizon:** **15 minutes**\n\n- **Trading style:** high-frequency directional decision making\n\n- **Primary success metric:** **directional accuracy >= 65%**\n\n- **Secondary success metric:** **more than 90 bets per month**\n\nThe system must optimize for both:\n\n1. **Prediction quality**: at least **65% directional accuracy**\n\n2. **Trading activity**: at least **90 valid bets per month**\n\nA model that is accurate but generates too few trades is not acceptable.  \n\nA model that generates many trades but fails to reach 65% directional accuracy is also not acceptable.\n\n---\n\n## Initial State (build first)\n\nS₀ = online continuous adaptive trading system which:\n\n- uses **online market data** for training and updating\n\n- uses **evolutionary model search** (mutation, crossover, replacement of weak models)\n\n- uses **strict walk-forward validation**\n\n- uses **online Sharpe filtering**\n\n- uses **horizon ensembles** H = {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} as contextual signals\n\n- uses **consensus gating** for signal generation\n\n- uses **time-series foundation models** and sequence models\n\n- is centered on the **main execution target = BTC up/down over the next 15 minutes**\n\n---\n\n## Core Prediction Task\n\nAt each decision point, the system must output one of:\n\n- **UP** = BTC is expected to be higher in 15 minutes\n\n- **DOWN** = BTC is expected to be lower in 15 minutes\n\n- optional: **NO TRADE** only if confidence is too low\n\nThe system should be designed to maximize profitable directional decisions while maintaining enough signal frequency to exceed **90 bets per month**.\n\n---\n\n## Optimization Targets\n\nThe system must optimize for the following target profile:\n\n- **Directional accuracy >= 65%**\n\n- **Bets per month > 90**\n\n- **Positive net profitability**\n\n- **Strong Sharpe ratio**\n\n- **Controlled drawdown**\n\n- **Robustness across market regimes**\n\n- **Stable out-of-sample performance**\n\n---\n\n## Fitness Priorities\n\nFitness should prioritize:\n\n1. **Directional accuracy on 15m BTC prediction**\n\n2. **Monthly bet count**\n\n3. **PnL**\n\n4. **Sharpe ratio**\n\n5. **Drawdown control**\n\n6. **Regime robustness**\n\n7. **Consistency across walk-forward folds**\n\nAny candidate that fails either:\n\n- **accuracy < 65%**\n\n- **bets/month <= 90**\n\nshould be considered below target even if other metrics look good.\n\n---\n\n## Data and Modeling Requirements\n\nThe system should:\n\n- learn from **BTC market structure**\n\n- exploit **short-term directional patterns**\n\n- use **multi-timescale context** while keeping the target fixed at 15m\n\n- adapt online as conditions change\n\n- discover alpha from:\n\n  - price action\n\n  - volatility\n\n  - momentum\n\n  - order-flow proxies\n\n  - volume\n\n  - derivatives context if available\n\n  - regime features\n\n  - cross-horizon agreement\n\nLonger horizons {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} should be used as **contextual ensemble inputs**, not as replacements for the core 15m target.\n\n---\n\n## Validation Rules\n\nThe system must use:\n\n- strict **temporal splits**\n\n- strict **walk-forward validation**\n\n- no lookahead bias\n\n- no leakage\n\n- no random split for final evaluation\n\n- evaluation on realistic trading frequency\n\n- explicit measurement of:\n\n  - accuracy\n\n  - number of bets\n\n  - PnL\n\n  - Sharpe\n\n  - drawdown\n\n  - regime-specific performance\n\nA candidate is only truly good if it remains strong in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.\n\n---\n\n## Evolution Strategy\n\nRun this loop continuously:\n\nloop t = 1..∞\n\n    S_t = design_or_modify(S_{t-1})   # implement the current design\n\n    O_t = run(S_t)                    # train, validate, and trade / paper trade\n\n    P_t = measure(O_t)                # evaluate accuracy, bets/month, Sharpe, PnL, drawdown, regime behavior\n\n    Δ_t = reflect(S_t, P_t)           # identify weaknesses and bottlenecks\n\n    S_{t+1} = improve(S_t, Δ_t)       # produce a better design\n\nend\n\n---\n\n## Reflection Requirements\n\nDuring reflection, explicitly diagnose:\n\n- why accuracy is below 65% if target is missed\n\n- why signal frequency is below 90 bets/month if target is missed\n\n- whether the consensus gate is too strict\n\n- whether the model is overfitting to specific regimes\n\n- whether the ensemble is improving or diluting edge\n\n- whether the system is sacrificing too much frequency for precision\n\n- whether the system is producing too many low-quality trades\n\n- whether thresholding should be tightened or loosened\n\nThe next design must directly address the identified weakness.\n\n---\n\n## Design Guidance\n\nThe system should search low-frequency swing system.  \n\nThis is not a generic forecasting system.  \n\nIt is a **BTC 15-minute directional trading system** that must be both:\n\n- **accurate enough** (>= 65%)\n\n- **active enough** (> 90 bets/month)\n\n---\n\n## True Success Condition\n\nThe process should continue until the system consistently achieves:\n\n- **BTC 15m directional accuracy >= 65%**\n\n- **more than 90 The validation window might be a tough regime (e.g., low volatility, choppy) that breaks models trained on earlier data.\n    43→   - The models' Sharpe on validation is extremely negative, indicating they are consistently wrong or losing due to costs outweighing moves.\n    44→\n    45→## Reflection & Planned Improvements (Step 7)\n    46→\n    47→The priority is to **stop the bleed** and find a configuration that yields positive Sharpe, even if accuracy or bet count temporarily drop (must stay >90 bets/mois).\n    48→\n    49→### A. Revert horizon features to base only (15m)\n    50→- Change `FeatureConfig.HORIZONS` back to `{\"15m\": 1}` (remove 1h).\n    51→- Rationale: Horizon features appear to cause overfitting and are not earning their keep. The HorizonEnsemble is broken and unused. Simplify to reduce feature space (~31 features) and improve generalization.\n    52→\n    53→### B. Tighten consensus gating further\n    54→- Increase `MIN_MODELS_AGREE` from 2 → 3 (require 3 out of 5 models to agree)\n    55→- Increase `MIN_CONFIDENCE` from 0.75 → 0.80\n    56→- Rationale: Drastically reduce signal frequency to only the highest-conviction trades. This should improve the per-trade edge and push Sharpe positive. With current 591 bets/month, even a 70% reduction would still yield ~177 bets/month, safely above 90.\n    57→\n    58→### C. Increase regularization and reduce model complexity\n    59→- For random forest: lower `RANDOM_FOREST_MAX_DEPTH` from 10 → 8\n    60→- Increase `RANDOM_FOREST_MIN_SAMPLES_LEAF` from 5 → 10\n    61→- Rationale: Simpler models may generalize better to recent data and reduce overfitting.\n    62→\n    63→### D. Fix horizon ensemble bug (optional)\n    64→- Investigate `processor.py:resample_to_horizon` and target creation for horizon to eliminate NaNs.\n    65→- But this is lower priority; we may abandon horizon ensemble entirely if base-only works.\n    66→\n    67→### E. Consider minimal horizon ensemble integration (if needed)\n    68→- If Step 7 still yields negative Sharpe with base-only features, we could integrate horizon predictions as an additional feature (one extra column: horizon consensus probability) rather than full feature expansion.\n    69→\n    70→## Technical Notes\n    71→- Bug fix already applied: `run_walk_forward` no longer evolves after final generation.\n    72→- Progress prints are sufficient for pm2 watchdog.\n    73→- Horizon ensemble training is broken; will skip until fixed.\n    74→\n    75→## Next Step (Step 7)\n    76→Implement the Step 7 configuration changes (A, B, C), run the system, and evaluate if Sharpe becomes positive while maintaining >90 bets/month and >65% accuracy.\n    77→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. Target: >=65% accuracy, >90 bets/month, positive Sharpe.\n\n## Current Status: ⚠️ Horizon Re-Enable Attempt 2 - Sharpe Still Negative\n\nStep 6 completed:\n- Applied consensus tightening: MIN_MODELS_AGREE=2, MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.75\n- Increased data/model capacity: POPULATION_SIZE=5, MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES=4000, ONLINE_DATA_DAYS=90\n- Kept 1-hour horizon features active.\n\n## Metrics from Step 6 Run (20260318_050319)\n```\nAccuracy:          0.8309  (83.1%)   ✅ >65% target\nBets/month:        591.41  ✅ >90 target\nSharpe:           -14.56   ❌ negative (improved from -28.57 but still negative)\nTotal return:      -1.27%\nMax drawdown:       1.5%\nN_trades:           ~? (high frequency)\nPopulation fitness: best=0.8845 (training)\n```\n\n## Diagnosis\n\n1. **Severe validation overfitting persists**\n   - Training fitness: 0.8845 (RF) and ~0.675 (logistic)\n   - Validation Sharpe for individuals: -189 to -173 (catastrophic)\n   - The expanded feature set (62 features) seems to cause models to fit noise that completely fails out-of-sample.\n\n2. **Horizon ensemble training failure**\n   - All 5 horizon 1h random forest models failed: \"Input y contains NaN.\"\n   - Indicates a bug in horizon target alignment or missing value handling.\n   - This ensemble is currently unused in signal generation, so only the horizon technical features are present. They may be contributing to overfitting without providing predictive power.\n\n3. **Consensus gating still too permissive for profitability**\n   - Even with MIN_MODELS_AGREE=2 and MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.75, bet count remains high (~591/month).\n   - Accuracy is high (83%), but after 0.15% round-trip costs (fee+slippage), returns are negative.\n   - Suggests many high-confidence predictions have very small directional edge (wins are tiny, losses are larger than wins or vice versa).\n\n4. **Potential regime shift**\n   - Training data ~41 days (4000 samples), validation 7 days. The validation window might be a tough regime (e.g., low volatility, choppy) that breaks models trained on earlier data.\n   - The models' Sharpe on validation is extremely negative, indicating they are consistently wrong or losing due to costs outweighing moves.\n\n## Reflection & Planned Improvements (Step 7)\n\nThe priority is to **stop the bleed** and find a configuration that yields positive Sharpe, even if accuracy or bet count temporarily drop (must stay >90 bets/mois).\n\n### A. Revert horizon features to base only (15m)\n- Change `FeatureConfig.HORIZONS` back to `{\"15m\": 1}` (remove 1h).\n- Rationale: Horizon features appear to cause overfitting and are not earning their keep. The HorizonEnsemble is broken and unused. Simplify to reduce feature space (~31 features) and improve generalization.\n\n### B. Tighten consensus gating further\n- Increase `MIN_MODELS_AGREE` from 2 → 3 (require 3 out of 5 models to agree)\n- Increase `MIN_CONFIDENCE` from 0.75 → 0.80\n- Rationale: Drastically reduce signal frequency to only the highest-conviction trades. This should improve the per-trade edge and push Sharpe positive. With current 591 bets/month, even a 70% reduction would still yield ~177 bets/month, safely above 90.\n\n### C. Increase regularization and reduce model complexity\n- For random forest: lower `RANDOM_FOREST_MAX_DEPTH` from 10 → 8\n- Increase `RANDOM_FOREST_MIN_SAMPLES_LEAF` from 5 → 10\n- Rationale: Simpler models may generalize better to recent data and reduce overfitting.\n\n### D. Fix horizon ensemble bug (optional)\n- Investigate `processor.py:resample_to_horizon` and target creation for horizon to eliminate NaNs.\n- But this is lower priority; we may abandon horizon ensemble entirely if base-only works.\n\n### E. Consider minimal horizon ensemble integration (if needed)\n- If Step 7 still yields negative Sharpe with base-only features, we could integrate horizon predictions as an additional feature (one extra column: horizon consensus probability) rather than full feature expansion.\n\n## Technical Notes\n- Bug fix already applied: `run_walk_forward` no longer evolves after final generation.\n- Progress prints are sufficient for pm2 watchdog.\n- Horizon ensemble training is broken; will skip until fixed.\n\n## Next Step (Step 7)\nImplement the Step 7 configuration changes (A, B, C), run the system, and evaluate if Sharpe becomes positive while maintaining >90 bets/month and >65% accuracy.\n","numLines":77,"startLine":1,"totalLines":77}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_fe6573a5074641c4a23bd96e","type":"tool_result","content":"# ArBos Trading System - State\n\n## Summary\nBTC 15-minute directional prediction system with evolutionary model search and walk-forward validation. Target: >=65% accuracy, >90 bets/month, positive Sharpe.\n\n## Current Status: ⚠️ Horizon Re-Enable Attempt 2 - Sharpe Still Negative\n\nStep 6 completed:\n- Applied consensus tightening: MIN_MODELS_AGREE=2, MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.75\n- Increased data/model capacity: POPULATION_SIZE=5, MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES=4000, ONLINE_DATA_DAYS=90\n- Kept 1-hour horizon features active.\n\n## Metrics from Step 6 Run (20260318_050319)\n```\nAccuracy:          0.8309  (83.1%)   ✅ >65% target\nBets/month:        591.41  ✅ >90 target\nSharpe:           -14.56   ❌ negative (improved from -28.57 but still negative)\nTotal return:      -1.27%\nMax drawdown:       1.5%\nN_trades:           ~? (high frequency)\nPopulation fitness: best=0.8845 (training)\n```\n\n## Diagnosis\n\n1. **Severe validation overfitting persists**\n   - Training fitness: 0.8845 (RF) and ~0.675 (logistic)\n   - Validation Sharpe for individuals: -189 to -173 (catastrophic)\n   - The expanded feature set (62 features) seems to cause models to fit noise that completely fails out-of-sample.\n\n2. **Horizon ensemble training failure**\n   - All 5 horizon 1h random forest models failed: \"Input y contains NaN.\"\n   - Indicates a bug in horizon target alignment or missing value handling.\n   - This ensemble is currently unused in signal generation, so only the horizon technical features are present. They may be contributing to overfitting without providing predictive power.\n\n3. **Consensus gating still too permissive for profitability**\n   - Even with MIN_MODELS_AGREE=2 and MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.75, bet count remains high (~591/month).\n   - Accuracy is high (83%), but after 0.15% round-trip costs (fee+slippage), returns are negative.\n   - Suggests many high-confidence predictions have very small directional edge (wins are tiny, losses are larger than wins or vice versa).\n\n4. **Potential regime shift**\n   - Training data ~41 days (4000 samples), validation 7 days. The validation window might be a tough regime (e.g., low volatility, choppy) that breaks models trained on earlier data.\n   - The models' Sharpe on validation is extremely negative, indicating they are consistently wrong or losing due to costs outweighing moves.\n\n## Reflection & Planned Improvements (Step 7)\n\nThe priority is to **stop the bleed** and find a configuration that yields positive Sharpe, even if accuracy or bet count temporarily drop (must stay >90 bets/mois).\n\n### A. Revert horizon features to base only (15m)\n- Change `FeatureConfig.HORIZONS` back to `{\"15m\": 1}` (remove 1h).\n- Rationale: Horizon features appear to cause overfitting and are not earning their keep. The HorizonEnsemble is broken and unused. Simplify to reduce feature space (~31 features) and improve generalization.\n\n### B. Tighten consensus gating further\n- Increase `MIN_MODELS_AGREE` from 2 → 3 (require 3 out of 5 models to agree)\n- Increase `MIN_CONFIDENCE` from 0.75 → 0.80\n- Rationale: Drastically reduce signal frequency to only the highest-conviction trades. This should improve the per-trade edge and push Sharpe positive. With current 591 bets/month, even a 70% reduction would still yield ~177 bets/month, safely above 90.\n\n### C. Increase regularization and reduce model complexity\n- For random forest: lower `RANDOM_FOREST_MAX_DEPTH` from 10 → 8\n- Increase `RANDOM_FOREST_MIN_SAMPLES_LEAF` from 5 → 10\n- Rationale: Simpler models may generalize better to recent data and reduce overfitting.\n\n### D. Fix horizon ensemble bug (optional)\n- Investigate `processor.py:resample_to_horizon` and target creation for horizon to eliminate NaNs.\n- But this is lower priority; we may abandon horizon ensemble entirely if base-only works.\n\n### E. Consider minimal horizon ensemble integration (if needed)\n- If Step 7 still yields negative Sharpe with base-only features, we could integrate horizon predictions as an additional feature (one extra column: horizon consensus probability) rather than full feature expansion.\n\n## Technical Notes\n- Bug fix already applied: `run_walk_forward` no longer evolves after final generation.\n- Progress prints are sufficient for pm2 watchdog.\n- Horizon ensemble training is broken; will skip until fixed.\n\n## Next Step (Step 7)\nImplement the Step 7 configuration changes (A, B, C), run the system, and evaluate if Sharpe becomes positive while maintaining >90 bets/month and >65% accuracy.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"37ae65e3-d294-4862-a472-e039814bfcf5","uuid":"990259fd-dd7e-4c24-9801-78f2d5eaa02c","timestamp":"2026-03-18T06:30:48.371Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"# ArBos Trading System - State\n\n## Summary\nBTC 15-minute directional prediction system with evolutionary model search and walk-forward validation. Load data from disk or fetch from Binance if not available/forced.\n    95→        \"\"\"\n    96→        if not force_refetch and self.data_path.exists():\n    97→            print(f\"Loading data from {self.data_path}\", flush=True)\n    98→            df = pd.read_csv(self.data_path, parse_dates=[\"open_time\"])\n    99→        else:\n   100→            print(\"Fetching fresh data from flush=True)\n   113→        return df\n   114→\n   115→    def prepare_features(self, df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n   116→        \"\"\"\n   117→        Prepare feature matrix X and target y.\n   118→        \"\"\"\n   119→        print(\"Preparing features...\", flush=True)\n   120→\n   121→        # Clean data\n   122→        df = clean_data(df)\n   123→\n   124→        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\n   125→        df = create_target(df, forward_periods=1)\n   126→\n   127→        # Generate horizon ensemble features (these will be added as extra columns)\n   128→ df\n   132→        df = df.merge(horizon_features, on=\"open_time\", how=\"left\")\n   133→\n   134→        # Drop rows with NaN in target\n   135→        df = df.dropna(subset=[\"target\"])\n \"close\", \"volume\",\n   145→                \"quote_volume\", \"trades_count\", \"future_close\", \"target\"\n   146→            ]\n   147→        ]\n   148→\n   149→        print(f\"Feature count: {len(self.feature_names)}\", flush=True)\n   150→        return df\n   151→\n   152→    def evolve_population(\n   153→        self,\n   154→        X_train: pd.DataFrame,\n   155→        y_train: pd.Series,\n   156→        generations: int = ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\n   157→    ) -> Population:\n   158→        \"\"\"\n   159→        Evolve a population of models.\n   160→        \"\"\"\n   161→        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n   162→\n   163→        pop = Population(\n   164→            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n   165→            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n   166→            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n   167→            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n   168→            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n   169→        )\n   170→\n   171→        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n   172→\n   173→        for gen in range(generations):\n   174→            print(f\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\", flush=True)\n   175→\n   176→            # Evaluate all individuals\n   177→            for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n   178→                try:\n   179→                    print(f\"    Training individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   180→                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n   181→                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\n   182→                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n   183→                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n   184→\n   185→                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n   186→                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n   187→                    print(f\"      -> fitness={ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n   188→                except Exception as e:\n   189→                    print(f\"      -> training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n   190→                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   191→\n   192→            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n   193→            if gen < generations - 1:\n   194→                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n   195→                pop.evolve()\n   196→\n   197→            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n   198→            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   199→\n   200→        return pop\n   201→\n   202→    def run_walk_forward(\n   203→        self,\n   204→        df: pd.DataFrame\n   205→    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n   206→        \"\"\"\n   207→        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n   208→        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n   209→        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n   210→        \"\"\"\n   211→        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n   212→\n   213→        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n   214→\n   215→        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n   216→        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n   217→        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n   218→\n   219→        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n   220→        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n   221→            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n   222→            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n   223→\n   224→        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n   225→        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n   226→        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n   227→\n   228→        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n   229→        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n   230→            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n   231→            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n   232→\n   233→        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n   234→\n   235→        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n   236→        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n   237→        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n   238→        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n   239→\n   240→        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n   241→        pop = Population(\n   242→            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n   243→            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n   244→            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n   245→            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n   246→            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n   247→        )\n   248→        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n   249→\n   250→        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n   251→            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n   252→            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n   253→            for ind in pop.individuals:\n   254→                try:\n   255→                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n   256→                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n   257→                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n   258→                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n   259→                except Exception as e:\n   260→                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n   261→                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   262→\n   263→            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n   264→            if gen < ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION - 1:\n   265→                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n   266→                pop.evolve()\n   267→                best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n   268→                print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   269→            else:\n   270→                # Final generation: no evolution, just report best\n   271→                best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n   272→                print(f\"    Final best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   273→\n   274→        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n   275→        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n   276→\n   277→        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n   278→        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n   279→        for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n   280→            # Skip individuals that failed training\n   281→            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n   282→                print(f\"  Skipping individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} (failed training)\", flush=True)\n   283→                continue\n   284→            try:\n   285→                print(f\"  Rescoring individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   286→                # Predict on validation\n   287→                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n   288→                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n   289→                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n   290→                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n   291→                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n   292→                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n   293→                # Run simulation\n   294→                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n   295→                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n   296→                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n   297→ if needed\n   300→                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n   301→            except Exception as e:\n   302→                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n   303→                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   304→\n   305→        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n   306→        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n   307→\n   308→        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n   309→        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n   310→            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n   311→            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n   312→        )\n   313→        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n   314→\n   315→        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n self.trader.metrics.copy()\n   321→\n   322→        # Compute raw accuracy\n   323→        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n   324→        if mask.any():\n   325→            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n   326→        else:\n   327→            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n   328→\n   329→        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n   330→        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n   331→\n   332→        return metrics\n   333→\n   334→    def train_best_models(\n   335→        self,\n   336→        df: pd.DataFrame,\n   337→        population: Population,\n   338→        horizon_data: dict = None\n 395→        self.top_models = top_models\n   396→\n   397→    def generate_current_signals(\n   398→        self,\n   399→        current_features: pd.DataFrame,\n   400→        use_horizon: bool = For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\n   428→        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\n   429→            cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\n   430→            df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff].copy()\n   431→            print(f\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\", flush=True)\n   432→\n   433→        # 3. Prepare features\n   434→        print(\"[main] Starting feature preparation...\", flush=True)\n   435→        df = self.prepare_features(df)\n   436→        print(f\"[main] Feature preparation complete: {len(df)} rows, {len(self.feature_names)} features\", flush=True)\n   437→\n   438→        # 3. Split into recent data for training and current for prediction\n   439→        # Use last 30 days for training, current for signal generation\n   440→        recent_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=30)\n   441→        train_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] <= recent_cutoff].copy()\n   442→        current_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] > recent_cutoff].copy()\n   443→\n   444→        # Limit training samples to control memory/time (use most recent samples)\n   445→        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n   446→            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n   447→            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n   448→\n   449→        if len(train_df) < ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n   450→            print(f\"Insufficient training data: {len(train_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n   451→            return None\n   452→\n   453→        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n   454→        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n   455→\n   456→        # 4. Evolve population\n   457→        population = self.evolve_population(X_train, y_train, generations=ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION)\n   458→\n   459→        # 5. Train best models on full recent data and prepare horizon ensemble\n   469→        self.train_best_models(df, population)\n   470→\n   471→        # 7. Generate current signals\n   472→        if len(current_df) > 0:\n   473→            current_features = current_df[self.feature_names]\n   474→            signals = self.generate_current_signals(current_features)\n   475→            print(f\"Generated {len(signals)} current signals\", flush=True)\n   476→            latest_signal = signals.iloc[-1] if len(signals) > 0 else None\n   477→            print(f\"Latest signal: {latest_signal} (1=UP, 0=DOWN, -1=NO TRADE)\", flush=True)\n   478→        else:\n   479→            signals = pd.Series()\n   480→            latest_signal = None\n   481→\n   482→        # 8. Save results\n   483→        self._save_results(metrics, population, signals)\n   484→\n   485→        # Compile full result\n   486→        result = {\n   487→            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n   488→            \"accuracy\": metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0),\n   489→            \"bets_per_month\": metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0),\n   490→            \"sharpe\": metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0),\n   491→            \"max_drawdown\": metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0),\n   492→            \"total_return\": metrics.get(\"total_return\", 0.0),\n   493→            \"latest_signal\": int(latest_signal) if latest_signal is not None else None,\n   494→            \"population_fitness\": {str(i): ind.fitness for i, ind in enumerate(population.get_best(5))}\n   495→        }\n   496→\n   497→        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n   498→        print(\"STEP COMPLETE\", flush=True)\n   499→        print(f\"Metrics: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}\", flush=True)\n   500→        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n   501→\n   502→        return result\n   503→\n   504→    def _save_results(self, metrics: dict, population: Population, signals: pd.Series):\n   505→        \"\"\"Save results to disk.\"\"\"\n   506→        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n   507→        run_dir = self.results_dir / timestamp\n   508→        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   509→\n   510→        # Save metrics\n   511→        with open(run_dir / \"metrics.json\", \"w\") as f:\n   512→            json.dump(metrics, f, indent=2)\n   513→\n   514→        # Save signals\n   515→        signals.to_csv(run_dir / \"signals.csv\")\n   516→\n   517→        # Save top models\n   518→        top_models = population.get_best(5)\n   519→        model_dir = run_dir / \"models\"\n   520→        model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n   521→        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n   522→            try:\n   523→                ind.model.save(str(model_dir / f\"model_{i}_{ind.model_type}.pkl\"))\n   524→            except Exception as e:\n   525→                print(f\"Could not save model {i}: {e}\", flush=True)\n   526→\n   527→        # Save population metadata\n   528→        pop_data = []\n   529→        for ind in population.individuals[:10]:\n   530→            pop_data.append({\n   531→                \"model_type\": ind.model_type,\n   532→                \"params\": ind.params,\n   533→                \"fitness\": float(ind.fitness)\n   534→            })\n   535→        with open(run_dir / \"population.json\", \"w\") as f:\n   536→            json.dump(pop_data, f, indent=2)\n   537→\n   538→        print(f\"Results saved to {run_dir}\", flush=True)\n   539→\n   540→\n   541→def main():\n   542→    \"\"\"Entry point for running the trading system.\"\"\"\n   543→    print(\"[main] main() started\", flush=True)\n   544→    system = TradingSystem()\n   545→\n   546→    # Run one step\n   547→    result = system.run_online_step()\n   548→\n   549→    if result:\n   550→        print(f\"\\nStep completed. Latest signal: {result['latest_signal']}\", flush=True)\n   551→        print(f\"Accuracy: {result['accuracy']:.4f}, Bets/month: {result['bets_per_month']:.2f}\", flush=True)\n   552→    else:\n   553→        print(\"Step did not complete successfully.\", flush=True)\n   554→\n   555→\n   556→if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n   557→    main()\n   558→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. Models' validation performance was poor (Sharpe ~ -240), so even 2-agree would likely still produce no trades. However, we need to generate trades to have any chance.\n2. **Model quality insufficient** — logistic models are near random (0.54 training accuracy). Random forests overfit (0.77 training accuracy but catastrophic out-of-sample Sharpe). Consider dropping logistic regression (next if needed)\n- If Step 8 still yields negative Sharpe despite bets >90, remove weak models from the population to improve average skill: set `MODEL_TYPES = [\"random_forest\"]` only.\n- Rationale: Logistic models harm consensus by disagreeing and adding noise.\n\n## Technical Notes\n- Horizon ensemble disabled (only base horizon). No horizon bug encountered.\n- Training runtime ~66 minutes; output sufficient for watchdog.\n- Current config already includes Step 7 regularization (RF max_depth=8, min_samples_leaf=10). Keep these.\n\n## Next Step (Step 8)\nApply consensus relaxation (MIN_MODELS_AGREE=2, MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.75) and rerun. Evaluate:","+- Bets/month > 90?","+- accuracy >= 65%?","+- Sharpe > 0?","+","+If targets still not met, proceed to drop logistic models in Step 9."]}],"originalFile":"# ArBos Trading System - State\n\n## Summary\nBTC 15-minute directional prediction system with evolutionary model search and walk-forward validation. Target: >=65% accuracy, >90 bets/month, positive Sharpe.\n\n## Current Status: ⚠️ Horizon Re-Enable Attempt 2 - Sharpe Still Negative\n\nStep 6 completed:\n- Applied consensus tightening: MIN_MODELS_AGREE=2, MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.75\n- Increased data/model capacity: POPULATION_SIZE=5, MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES=4000, ONLINE_DATA_DAYS=90\n- Kept 1-hour horizon features active.\n\n## Metrics from Step 6 Run (20260318_050319)\n```\nAccuracy:          0.8309  (83.1%)   ✅ >65% target\nBets/month:        591.41  ✅ >90 target\nSharpe:           -14.56   ❌ negative (improved from -28.57 but still negative)\nTotal return:      -1.27%\nMax drawdown:       1.5%\nN_trades:           ~? (high frequency)\nPopulation fitness: best=0.8845 (training)\n```\n\n## Diagnosis\n\n1. **Severe validation overfitting persists**\n   - Training fitness: 0.8845 (RF) and ~0.675 (logistic)\n   - Validation Sharpe for individuals: -189 to -173 (catastrophic)\n   - The expanded feature set (62 features) seems to cause models to fit noise that completely fails out-of-sample.\n\n2. **Horizon ensemble training failure**\n   - All 5 horizon 1h random forest models failed: \"Input y contains NaN.\"\n   - Indicates a bug in horizon target alignment or missing value handling.\n   - This ensemble is currently unused in signal generation, so only the horizon technical features are present. They may be contributing to overfitting without providing predictive power.\n\n3. **Consensus gating still too permissive for profitability**\n   - Even with MIN_MODELS_AGREE=2 and MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.75, bet count remains high (~591/month).\n   - Accuracy is high (83%), but after 0.15% round-trip costs (fee+slippage), returns are negative.\n   - Suggests many high-confidence predictions have very small directional edge (wins are tiny, losses are larger than wins or vice versa).\n\n4. **Potential regime shift**\n   - Training data ~41 days (4000 samples), validation 7 days. The validation window might be a tough regime (e.g., low volatility, choppy) that breaks models trained on earlier data.\n   - The models' Sharpe on validation is extremely negative, indicating they are consistently wrong or losing due to costs outweighing moves.\n\n## Reflection & Planned Improvements (Step 7)\n\nThe priority is to **stop the bleed** and find a configuration that yields positive Sharpe, even if accuracy or bet count temporarily drop (must stay >90 bets/mois).\n\n### A. Revert horizon features to base only (15m)\n- Change `FeatureConfig.HORIZONS` back to `{\"15m\": 1}` (remove 1h).\n- Rationale: Horizon features appear to cause overfitting and are not earning their keep. The HorizonEnsemble is broken and unused. Simplify to reduce feature space (~31 features) and improve generalization.\n\n### B. Tighten consensus gating further\n- Increase `MIN_MODELS_AGREE` from 2 → 3 (require 3 out of 5 models to agree)\n- Increase `MIN_CONFIDENCE` from 0.75 → 0.80\n- Rationale: Drastically reduce signal frequency to only the highest-conviction trades. This should improve the per-trade edge and push Sharpe positive. With current 591 bets/month, even a 70% reduction would still yield ~177 bets/month, safely above 90.\n\n### C. Increase regularization and reduce model complexity\n- For random forest: lower `RANDOM_FOREST_MAX_DEPTH` from 10 → 8\n- Increase `RANDOM_FOREST_MIN_SAMPLES_LEAF` from 5 → 10\n- Rationale: Simpler models may generalize better to recent data and reduce overfitting.\n\n### D. Fix horizon ensemble bug (optional)\n- Investigate `processor.py:resample_to_horizon` and target creation for horizon to eliminate NaNs.\n- But this is lower priority; we may abandon horizon ensemble entirely if base-only works.\n\n### E. Consider minimal horizon ensemble integration (if needed)\n- If Step 7 still yields negative Sharpe with base-only features, we could integrate horizon predictions as an additional feature (one extra column: horizon consensus probability) rather than full feature expansion.\n\n## Technical Notes\n- Bug fix already applied: `run_walk_forward` no longer evolves after final generation.\n- Progress prints are sufficient for pm2 watchdog.\n- Horizon ensemble training is broken; will skip until fixed.\n\n## Next Step (Step 7)\nImplement the Step 7 configuration changes (A, B, C), run the system, and evaluate if Sharpe becomes positive while maintaining >90 bets/month and >65% accuracy.\n"}}
